openmeter/cost.purpose: Computes feature cost by querying ClickHouse meter usage via streaming.Connector and resolving per-unit costs from feature configuration (manual or LLM-based). Two-layer domain: adapter/ owns ClickHouse queries and cost resolution, service/ owns input validation and delegation.

patterns.name: Service validates then delegates
patterns.description: cost.Service.QueryFeatureCost calls input.Validate() then cost.Adapter.QueryFeatureCost — no business logic or data access in the service layer.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return s.adapter.QueryFeatureCost(ctx, input)

patterns.name: Pre-resolve LLM prices once per query
patterns.description: The adapter builds a price cache keyed on (provider, model) via getLLMPrices before iterating rows — never call llmcostService.ResolvePrice inside the per-row loop.
patterns.example: prices, err := a.getLLMPrices(ctx, namespace, feature); // then pass prices to resolveUnitCost

patterns.name: Clone GroupBy/FilterGroupBy before merging
patterns.description: Never mutate the caller's streaming.QueryParams.GroupBy or FilterGroupBy; clone before injecting internal keys.
patterns.example: params.GroupBy = slices.Clone(input.QueryParams.GroupBy)

patterns.name: pricing-not-found → detail string, not hard error
patterns.description: In costResolverFunc, convert models.IsGenericNotFoundError to a detail string so the row is still emitted rather than aborting the query.
patterns.example: if models.IsGenericNotFoundError(err) { /* populate row.Detail instead of returning err */ }

patterns.name: QueryFeatureCostInput.Validate via NewNillableGenericValidationError
patterns.description: All cost input types validate using models.NewNillableGenericValidationError over errors.Join.
patterns.example: return models.NewNillableGenericValidationError(errors.Join(errs...))

key_file_guides.role: Defines cost.Adapter interface with the single method QueryFeatureCost; the contract for the adapter/ sub-package.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Keep this as the contract only; implementation lives in adapter/.

key_file_guides.role: Defines cost.Service interface plus all input/output types (QueryFeatureCostInput, CostQueryResult, CostQueryRow, ResolvedUnitCost) with Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Replicate NewNillableGenericValidationError for any new input type; cost is alpacadecimal and currency currencyx.Code.

anti_patterns: Calling llmcostService.ResolvePrice inside the per-row cost loop instead of pre-resolving via getLLMPrices
Passing *entdb.Client or touching Ent — this domain is ClickHouse-only via streaming.Connector
Mutating the caller's streaming.QueryParams.GroupBy/FilterGroupBy in place instead of cloning
Returning a hard error for pricing-not-found in costResolverFunc instead of a detail string
Adding computation or data access in service methods — delegate entirely to cost.Adapter

decisions.decision: Price cache keyed on (provider, model), not (provider, model, token_type)
decisions.rationale: Token-type breakdown happens inside resolveUnitCost after the cache lookup; caching at the coarser key reduces LLM-cost service calls.

decisions.decision: costResolverFunc is a function type, not an interface
decisions.rationale: Keeps resolution strategy as a swappable closure (LLM vs. manual) without a named type.

openmeter/credit.purpose: Manages credit grants and balance snapshots for metered entitlements. CreditConnector (= BalanceConnector + GrantConnector) is the public facade; engine/ computes burn-down without I/O; adapter/ persists via transaction-aware Ent repos; grant/balance/hook/driver sub-packages own the domain contract, snapshots, lifecycle hook, and v1 HTTP layer. Primary constraint: all effective times are truncated to Granularity (time.Minute).

patterns.name: transaction.Run wraps all multi-step writes
patterns.description: CreateGrant/VoidGrant/ResetUsageForOwner use transaction.Run then m.GrantRepo.WithTx(ctx, tx) to stay on the ctx-bound transaction.
patterns.example: transaction.Run(ctx, m.GrantRepo, func(ctx) (*grant.Grant, error) { tx, _ := entutils.GetDriverFromContext(ctx); return m.GrantRepo.WithTx(ctx, tx).CreateGrant(ctx, inp) })

patterns.name: LockOwnerForTx before any write
patterns.description: All mutating connector methods call m.OwnerConnector.LockOwnerForTx(ctx, ownerID, true) before writing grants/snapshots to prevent concurrent balance races.
patterns.example: err = m.OwnerConnector.LockOwnerForTx(ctx, ownerID, true)

patterns.name: GetLastValidSnapshotAt falls back to start-of-measurement
patterns.description: On NoSavedBalanceForOwnerError, create a zero-balance snapshot from GetStartOfMeasurement + NewStartingMap(grants) — never propagate the not-found error.
patterns.example: if _, ok := lo.ErrorsAs[*balance.NoSavedBalanceForOwnerError](err); ok { bal = balance.Snapshot{At: startOfMeasurement, ...} }

patterns.name: buildEngineForOwner caches period boundaries
patterns.description: Build a period cache from SortedPeriodsFromDedupedTimes before constructing the engine; resolve GetUsagePeriodStartAt from the cache inside the UsageQuerier closure, never per usage query.
patterns.example: periodCache := SortedPeriodsFromDedupedTimes(times)

patterns.name: Granularity truncation on all times
patterns.description: All effective and reset times are truncated to m.Granularity (time.Minute) before engine use and before storing.
patterns.example: input.EffectiveAt = input.EffectiveAt.Truncate(time.Minute)

patterns.name: Snapshotting acquires non-blocking lock and skips on failure
patterns.description: snapshotEngineResult uses a non-blocking LockOwnerForTx(false); failure to acquire must not abort the balance query that triggered it.
patterns.example: if err := transaction.RunWithNoValue(...LockOwnerForTx(ctx, owner, false)); err != nil { return nil }

patterns.name: ValidationIssue errors with HTTP status attribute
patterns.description: Validation errors use models.NewValidationIssue + commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute; CreateGrantInput.Validate uses ErrGrantAmountMustBePositive.WithAttr.
patterns.example: var ErrGrantAmountMustBePositive = models.NewValidationIssue(ErrCodeGrantAmountMustBePositive, "amount must be positive", models.WithFieldString("amount"), commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute(http.StatusBadRequest))

key_file_guides.role: Defines CreditConnector (= BalanceConnector + GrantConnector), CreditConnectorConfig (embedded by value), and NewCreditConnector.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreditConnectorConfig is embedded by value — new config fields are visible to all methods automatically.

key_file_guides.role: Implements BalanceConnector: GetBalanceAt, GetBalanceForPeriod, ResetUsageForOwner, GetLastValidSnapshotAt.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetBalanceAt/GetBalanceForPeriod truncate-up to the next minute (FIXME) — replicate in any new method. Cannot reset in the future or before the current usage period start.

key_file_guides.role: Implements GrantConnector: CreateGrant, VoidGrant; defines GrantConnector interface and CreateGrantInput.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateGrant truncates EffectiveAt and Recurrence.Anchor, locks the owner, then invalidates snapshots via BalanceSnapshotService.InvalidateAfter and publishes grant.NewCreatedEventV2FromGrant.

key_file_guides.role: Engine-building helpers: buildEngineForOwner, snapshotEngineResult, saveSnapshot, populate/removeInactiveGrants, SortedPeriodsFromDedupedTimes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: removeInactiveGrantsFromSnapshotAt errors if a grant in the snapshot balances map is not in the grants slice — pass the full grants slice used for engine.Run.

key_file_guides.role: Package-level ValidationIssue error vars with HTTP status attributes (ErrGrantAmountMustBePositive, ErrGrantEffectiveAtMustBeSet).
key_file_guides.watch_for: New validation errors must follow NewValidationIssue + WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute.

key_file_guides.role: ctrace singleton with typed OTel span options (WithOwner, WithPeriod, WithEngineParams).
key_file_guides.watch_for: All new connector methods should open a span via m.Tracer.Start and use cTrace helpers.

anti_patterns: Calling GrantRepo or BalanceSnapshotService methods without wrapping in transaction.Run during a multi-step write — bypasses the ctx-bound transaction
Omitting LockOwnerForTx before any grant/snapshot mutation — causes balance races under concurrency
Propagating balance.NoSavedBalanceForOwnerError to callers — always fall back to start-of-measurement snapshot
Querying GetUsagePeriodStartAt inside the QueryUsageFn callback at runtime — build the period cache in buildEngineForOwner instead
Passing the caller's grants slice to the engine without populating missing grants via populateBalanceSnapshotWithMissingGrantsActiveAt

decisions.decision: Period cache built once in buildEngineForOwner, resolved in-memory by the UsageQuerier closure
decisions.rationale: QueryUsageFn is called many times during engine.Run; caching period start times avoids O(n) DB calls per burn phase.

decisions.decision: Snapshotting acquires a non-blocking lock and silently skips on failure
decisions.rationale: Snapshotting is an optimisation; lock-acquisition failure must not abort the triggering balance query.

decisions.decision: All times truncated to Granularity (time.Minute) before engine use
decisions.rationale: ClickHouse stores events in minute-window chunks; sub-minute precision causes incorrect burn-down.

openmeter/currencies.purpose: Manages custom currencies and fiat-exchange cost bases for billing namespaces. Two-layer domain: adapter/ owns Ent/PostgreSQL persistence with full TransactingRepo discipline; service/ merges in-memory GOBL fiat enumeration with DB-stored custom currencies.

patterns.name: Input Validate() before transaction.Run
patterns.description: Service calls params.Validate() before transaction.Run; DB constraint errors are a last resort, not the primary validation gate.
patterns.example: if err := params.Validate(); err != nil { return Currency{}, err }; return transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx) (Currency, error) { return s.adapter.CreateCurrency(ctx, params) })

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping every adapter method
patterns.description: Every adapter method body is wrapped with entutils.TransactingRepo/TransactingRepoWithNoValue to honor the ctx-bound transaction.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx, tx *adapter) (Currency, error) { row, err := tx.db.Currency.Create()...; return mapCurrencyFromDB(row), err })

patterns.name: entdb.IsConstraintError → GenericConflictError
patterns.description: Adapter maps Ent constraint violations to models.NewGenericConflictError, never raw Ent errors.
patterns.example: if entdb.IsConstraintError(err) { return Currency{}, models.NewGenericConflictError(err) }

patterns.name: In-memory fiat enumeration via GOBL
patterns.description: Fiat currencies are listed from GOBL at runtime (not the DB); in-memory merge and pagination of fiat + custom sets lives in the service.
patterns.example: fiatCurrencies := gobl.AllCurrencies(); result := mergeAndPaginate(fiatCurrencies, customCurrencies, params.Page)

patterns.name: models.Validator assertion on every input type
patterns.description: All input structs declare var _ models.Validator = (*XInput)(nil) and implement Validate() with errors.Join.
patterns.example: var _ models.Validator = (*ListCurrenciesInput)(nil)

key_file_guides.role: Defines Adapter (CurrenciesAdapter + entutils.TxCreator) and the CurrenciesAdapter sub-interface.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adapter embeds TxCreator — any implementation must provide Tx/WithTx/Self.

key_file_guides.role: All domain types (Currency, CostBasis) and input types (ListCurrenciesInput, CreateCurrencyInput, CreateCostBasisInput, ListCostBasesInput) with Validate() and CurrencyType enum.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Add var _ models.Validator assertion for any new input type; Rate uses alpacadecimal and must be positive.

key_file_guides.role: Defines CurrencyService interface — the public facade for HTTP handlers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Keep in sync with adapter methods it delegates to; fiat-vs-custom logic lives in service/, not the adapter.

anti_patterns: Calling a.db.X directly without entutils.TransactingRepo — bypasses ctx-bound transactions
Importing openmeter/ent/db in the service layer — all DB access must go through the currencies.Adapter interface
Storing fiat currencies in the DB — they are enumerated in-memory from GOBL and merged at query time
Returning raw Ent errors — map constraint errors to GenericConflictError and not-found to GenericNotFoundError
Adding temporal business rules (future-date enforcement, defaulting) inside the adapter — they belong in the service

decisions.decision: Fiat currency list sourced in-memory from GOBL, not stored in the DB
decisions.rationale: Fiat currencies are a stable enumeration; storing them would require migrations on standard updates and create drift risk.

decisions.decision: Tx/WithTx/Self triad inlined on the adapter struct, not a shared TxWrapper
decisions.rationale: Currencies is a small domain; a single adapter file mirrors the pattern used across other small domain adapters.

openmeter/debug.purpose: Exposes a single ClickHouse-backed debug metrics endpoint returning per-subject event counts in OpenMetrics (Prometheus) format. Minimal two-file domain: debug.go owns the DebugConnector wrapping streaming.Connector; httpdriver/ translates HTTP to domain calls.

patterns.name: DebugConnector as the only service interface
patterns.description: Business logic lives in debugConnector wrapping streaming.Connector; httpdriver delegates to DebugConnector, never to streaming.Connector directly.
patterns.example: connector := debug.NewDebugConnector(streamingConnector); handler := httpdriver.NewHandler(connector, ...)

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs for every endpoint
patterns.description: Handlers use httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs (not http.Handler); namespace resolved via namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder.
patterns.example: httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(func(ctx, r) (string, error) { return namespaceDecoder(ctx) }, operationFn, encodeResponse)

patterns.name: Per-handler error encoder via AppendOptions
patterns.description: Error encoders are appended with httptransport.AppendOptions rather than replacing h.options.
patterns.example: opts := httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(myEncoder))

key_file_guides.role: DebugConnector interface and debugConnector implementation that queries streaming.CountEvents and formats Prometheus OpenMetrics output (openmeter_events_total counter).
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetDebugMetrics queries from start-of-day UTC (hardcoded window); document the query window for any new metrics method.
Returns plain-text OpenMetrics, not JSON; do not swap to a JSON encoder without updating Content-Type handling.

anti_patterns: Implementing http.Handler directly instead of httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs
Reading namespace from URL path/query instead of namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder
Replacing h.options instead of appending with httptransport.AppendOptions
Putting metrics computation inside the decode/encode functions — delegate to DebugConnector
Adding streaming.Connector as a direct dependency in httpdriver — always go through DebugConnector

decisions.decision: DebugConnector wraps streaming.Connector rather than exposing streaming directly
decisions.rationale: Keeps httpdriver decoupled from ClickHouse query details; DebugConnector owns the Prometheus format conversion.

openmeter/dedupe.purpose: Provides CloudEvent deduplication for the sink worker via the Deduplicator interface. Two implementations: memorydedupe/ (LRU, no-dependency fallback) and redisdedupe/ (TTL-based, pipelined batch, three key-format modes for zero-downtime migration). All callers depend on dedupe.Deduplicator; implementations are swapped by config.

patterns.name: Deduplicator interface as the contract boundary
patterns.description: All callers depend on dedupe.Deduplicator; implementations are swapped by config without callers knowing which backend is active.
patterns.example: var d dedupe.Deduplicator = memorydedupe.New(size) // or redisdedupe.New(cfg)

patterns.name: Item.Key() as the canonical dedup key
patterns.description: All implementations key their stores on item.Key() = "namespace-source-id"; never use raw event IDs or namespaces alone.
patterns.example: key := item.Key() // "myns-mysource-event123"

patterns.name: SET NX + TTL for Redis atomic set-if-not-exists
patterns.description: redisdedupe uses SetArgs{NX: true, TTL: d.ttl} to write-if-absent; never plain SET which overwrites.
patterns.example: d.Redis.SetArgs(ctx, key, val, redis.SetArgs{NX: true, TTL: d.ttl})

patterns.name: ContainsOrAdd for atomic check-and-set in memorydedupe
patterns.description: IsUnique uses LRU.ContainsOrAdd rather than separate Contains + Add to avoid TOCTOU races under concurrent ingest.
patterns.example: existed, _ := lru.ContainsOrAdd(item.Key(), nil)

patterns.name: Three-mode key format with explicit migration mode
patterns.description: redisdedupe supports rawkey, keyhash, keyhash-migration; every key-operation method must switch on d.mode.
patterns.example: switch d.mode { case ModeRawKey: return item.Key(); case ModeKeyHash: return GetKeyHash(item); ... }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Deduplicator interface, Item, Item.Key(), CheckUniqueBatchResult.
key_file_guides.watch_for: IsUnique is marked TODO/deprecated in favor of CheckUnique + Set; prefer the newer methods in new code.

key_file_guides.role: LRU-backed Deduplicator; IsUnique uses ContainsOrAdd for atomic check-and-set.
key_file_guides.watch_for: LRU stores nil values keyed by item.Key() — do not store event payload data.

key_file_guides.role: Redis-backed Deduplicator with pipelined batch writes and NX TTL semantics.
key_file_guides.watch_for: redis.Nil in pipeline results signals a pre-existing key (already deduped), not an error; guard d.Redis for nil before any call.
Changing the hash algorithm requires a new migration mode and key rotation plan — never change silently.

anti_patterns: Using Redis SET without NX — overwrites existing keys and defeats deduplication
Adding a new DedupeMode without updating every method's switch statement in redisdedupe
Ignoring redis.Nil in pipeline results — it signals a pre-existing key, not an error
Storing event payload data in the LRU cache — only nil values, keyed by item.Key()
Skipping Close() implementation on a new Deduplicator — must exist even as a no-op to satisfy the interface

decisions.decision: LRU eviction in memorydedupe over unbounded map growth
decisions.rationale: The sink worker processes high-volume events; unbounded growth would exhaust memory on long-running sinks.

decisions.decision: Three-mode key format (rawkey, keyhash, keyhash-migration) in redisdedupe
decisions.rationale: Changing the key format without a migration mode would leave pre-existing keys unreachable during rollout.

decisions.decision: xxh3-128 + base64url over SHA-224 or raw composite strings
decisions.rationale: xxh3 is much faster for high-throughput batch hashing; base64url keeps keys URL-safe and compact.
